c74object.name: tap.808.tom~
c74object.digest: TR-808-style tom/conga voice, synthesized from the analog circuit
c74object.description: The tap.808.tom~ object is a circuit-informed model of the Roland TR-808's tom/conga channels: a bridged-T resonator whose attack rings sharp and falls in pitch as it damps (the hardware's diode behavior), with the panel tuning knob sweeping the documented span for each size — low/mid/high toms cover roughly 80-100, 120-160, and 165-220 Hz; congas 165-220, 250-310, and 370-455 Hz. Toms add the hardware's pink-noise "artificial reverberation" layer; congas ring pure — the model attribute is the hardware's switch. Trigger with a signal rising edge (amplitude 0..1 = accent) or bang / trigger messages.

inlet.digest: Trigger: a rising edge fires the voice; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent

method.name: signal
method.description: A rising edge triggers the voice, sample-accurately; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent.

method.name: bang
method.description: Trigger at full accent (from the scheduler).

method.name: trigger
method.description: Trigger with an accent value (0..1, default 1).

attribute.name: size
attribute.digest: Channel size: low, mid, or high
attribute.description: Which of the hardware's three channels (default low).

attribute.name: model
attribute.description: The hardware's switch: tom (default; with the noise layer) or conga (pure ring, tuned higher).

attribute.name: tuning
attribute.description: 0..1 (default 0.5), sweeping the documented span for the selected size and model — e.g. mid tom 120..160 Hz.

attribute.name: seed
attribute.description: Seed (>= 1) for the tom noise layer; give mc. instances different seeds.
